description: A performance-focused analysis of AI image detection paradigms, revised for evidence-based accuracy, reliability, and integration with Adaptive Forensic Agent (AFA) frameworks. This version (1.4) distinguishes between paper-stated facts and analytical inferences, clarifies contexts, adds AIGC-specific ratings, and includes supported features for rule-based model selection in AIGC forensics. Incorporated empirical F1 scores from multi-dataset testing (e.g., AIGCDetect, GenImage) to optimize ratings and decision guidance.

profiles.core_mechanism: Segments an image into patches and shuffles them to disrupt global semantic structures. This procedure forces the classifier to learn discriminative features from local, low-level generator artifacts rather than overfitting to high-level scene content.
profiles.primary_use_case: Demonstrates high generalization accuracy across images with unseen semantic scenes. The methodology is designed to be independent of the image's global content, making it suitable for scenarios where subject matter is unpredictable.

profiles.core_mechanism: Utilizes contrastive learning across four image categories: Real, Fake, Real-Reconstructed, and Fake-Reconstructed. The reconstruction of real images creates challenging 'hard positive' samples, forcing the model to learn subtle, intrinsic fingerprints of a specific generative architecture.
profiles.primary_use_case: Designed to learn intrinsic architectural fingerprints of generative models, particularly Diffusion Models, enabling strong generalization to unseen generators of the same class (e.g., from one Diffusion Model to another).

profiles.core_mechanism: Uses a frozen CLIP ViT (preferably ViT-L/14) to extract CLS tokens from all intermediate blocks; concatenates them into a b×n×d tensor, projects to d′ (Q1), and applies a Trainable Importance Estimator (TIE) that softmax-weights features across blocks per feature dimension for per-feature cross-layer aggregation, yielding a b×d′ embedding. A second projection (Q2) and a small classifier output the fake/real logit. Trains only the lightweight head (Q1, TIE, Q2, classifier) with BCE + supervised contrastive loss (ξ≈0.1–0.8). Artifact-preserving preprocessing: Gaussian blur/JPEG (p=0.5), random crop 224, flip (p=0.5); test with center crop; no resizing. Ablations show intermediate-layer signals drive most gains; TIE further boosts by emphasizing artifact-rich mid-level blocks; SupContrast adds modest consistency. Robust under cropping/compression, reasonable under blur/noise; ViT-L/14 > ViT-B/32. Trained on ProGAN, generalizes broadly; adding diffusion-origin data markedly improves detection of commercial diffusion outputs.
profiles.primary_use_case: Rapid, cross-generator synthetic image detection with minimal compute/data (1-epoch head training), strong generalization to unseen GAN/Diffusion models, and robustness to common social-media operations (especially cropping/compression). Recommended as a default detector for large-scale moderation, forensic triage, and on-device screening where latency and simplicity matter. Guidance: use frozen CLIP ViT-L/14; train the head with BCE+SupContrast and artifact-preserving augments; start with ProGAN for broad coverage, add diffusion data for DALL·E/Midjourney/SD targets; default threshold 0.5 with optional calibration for domain shifts; avoid resizing to retain traces. Fits AFA pipelines as a low-footprint detector whose embeddings support rule-based routing or ensembling with frequency/artifact-based models.

profiles.core_mechanism: Models the invariant frequency distribution of real images via masked frequency reconstruction in a ViT backbone. At inference, computes Spectral Reconstruction Similarity (cosine consistency) between original, low-pass, and high-pass views across transformer blocks/tokens, contextualizes these signals with a Spectral Context Vector, and fuses patch-level cues using Spectral Context Attention to support native-resolution inputs. Synthetic images show weaker reconstruction-consistency and are detected as out-of-distribution.
profiles.primary_use_case: Generalized detection of AI-generated images from unseen GAN/Diffusion models at arbitrary resolutions, robust to common online perturbations (compression, blur, noise, resizing). Best suited for scenarios where subtle spectral inconsistencies—rather than semantics—drive reliable verification.

model_evaluation.description: Model Performance Evaluation in GenImage AIGC Detection Dataset(In-domain)

prediction.description: Model performance on original prediction probabilities.

calibration.description: Model performance on calibrated probabilities. The calibration set is GenImage itself.
